title: hibbertia huttii
intro: Hibbertia huttii, also known as the New Zealand Guinea flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae, native to New Zealand. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Hibbertia huttii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Hibbertia huttii can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, and is often used in traditional Maori medicine to treat various health conditions.
